recaps.text: Three children move with their parents to a cottage in the countryside and find a mysterious wood close by, where the trees seem to whisper among themselves. Deep inside grows an enormous tree, so tall its top is lost in the clouds. The children begin to climb it and discover little doors and windows set into the great trunk, realising this is no ordinary wood at all.

Climbing the Faraway Tree, the children make friends with the folk who live in its trunk: round-faced Moon-Face with his spiralling slippery-slip, golden-haired Silky the elf, the bad-tempered Angry Pixie, and busy Dame Washalot, whose wash-water they must dodge. At the very top, a hole in a cloud leads up into a land that changes from time to time. They ride the slippery-slip, sample magical treats, and meet the clanking, half-deaf Saucepan Man, learning that each new land can be wonderful or risky.

The children visit a string of strange countries perched above the tree, from a whirling Roundabout Land to lands full of toys and treats, always careful to climb back down before the top swings away to somewhere new. Not every land is friendly, and there are scrapes to be got out of - at one point facing trouble from an army of red goblins - with Moon-Face, Silky and the Saucepan Man ready to help.

After many adventures in the lands above the Faraway Tree, including a visit to the delightful Land of Birthdays, the children come safely home again. They have gained a set of magical friends in the tree and learned how its shifting lands work, ready for whenever they can next slip back into the Enchanted Wood.
